title: Quiz: Future Directions in Quantum Chemistry
description: Test your understanding of emerging technologies

questions.question: What is the main advantage quantum computers could offer for chemistry calculations?

options.text: Polynomial scaling instead of exponential for electron correlation

questions.explanation: Classical computers face exponential scaling for exact electron correlation (2^N for N electrons). Quantum computers can potentially represent quantum states naturally, achieving polynomial scaling. This could enable calculations currently impossible for strongly correlated systems.

questions.question: What is the VQE (Variational Quantum Eigensolver) algorithm primarily used for?

options.text: Finding ground state energies on near-term quantum computers

questions.explanation: VQE is a hybrid quantum-classical algorithm designed for NISQ (Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum) devices. It variationally optimizes a parameterized quantum circuit to find molecular ground state energies, making it practical for current noisy quantum hardware.

questions.question: What is 'active learning' in the context of computational chemistry?

options.text: Running calculations during business hours only
Iteratively selecting which calculations to run based on model uncertainty

questions.explanation: Active learning iteratively selects which data points (calculations) to add based on where the current model is most uncertain. This efficiently explores chemical space with 10× fewer calculations than random sampling while achieving the same model accuracy.

questions.question: What does a 'self-driving laboratory' integrate?

options.text: AI planning, robotic execution, and computational analysis in a closed loop

questions.explanation: Self-driving laboratories combine AI/ML for experiment planning, robotic systems for execution, automated instruments for measurement, and computational analysis to learn from results and propose the next experiment - all in a closed loop with minimal human intervention.

questions.question: Current quantum computers (2024) can already solve chemistry problems faster than classical supercomputers for industrially relevant molecules.
questions.explanation: False. Current NISQ devices are limited to small demonstrations (H₂, LiH, BeH₂). Quantum advantage for practical chemistry problems is still years away, pending development of error correction and larger, less noisy quantum processors. Classical methods remain more practical for now.

questions.question: Which skills are increasingly important for computational chemistry careers? (Select all)
questions.explanation: Modern computational chemistry careers require interdisciplinary skills: ML/AI for accelerating predictions, programming for automation and analysis, and communication to work with experimentalists, engineers, and business stakeholders. Domain expertise (chemistry) remains foundational.
